notes: Proto-Germanic gutaz → EN good / DE gut
Proto-Germanic lubją → EN love / DE Liebe
Proto-Germanic hailagaz → EN holy / DE heilig
Proto-Germanic starkaz → EN stark / DE stark
from causa → because/porque/weil — marks reason clauses
from tempus → when/quando/wenn/quand — marks time clauses
from optimus → ótimo/optimal/best — positive superlative scale
from pessimus → péssimo/worst/pésimo — negative extreme scale
CJK ideogram 愛 — shared in ZH/JA(ai)/KO(ae) — same root as amor
CJK ideogram 悲 — shared in ZH/JA(hi)/KO(bi) — same root as tristis
CJK ideogram 喜 — shared in ZH/JA(ki)/KO(hui) — same root as felix
CJK ideogram 怒 — shared in ZH/JA(do)/KO(no) — same root as ira
CJK ideogram 美 — shared in ZH/JA(bi)/KO(mi) — same root as bellus
CJK ideogram 善 — shared in ZH/JA(zen)/KO(seon) — same root as bonus
CJK ideogram 悪 — shared in ZH/JA(aku)/KO(ak) — same root as malus
CJK compound 希(rare)+望(hope/desire) — shared ZH/JA(kibou) — same root as sperare
Proto-Germanic *fug — fuck/foda/foder/fotre — vulgar sexual act; doubles as intensifier
from Latin puta (girl/whore) — puta/putain/puttana — strongest negative intensifier in Romance
from aqua — água/agua/acqua/eau — universal water root
Tupi y=water; root of Iguaçu(great water)/Igarapé/Ipiranga
Tupi Tupã=thunder god; sacred root of Tupinambá people
Tupi abá=person; root of Tupinambá(real people)
Sanskrit sukha — antonym of dukkha; Buddhist/Hindu core concept
Sanskrit dukkha — fundamental Buddhist concept of suffering
Sanskrit dharma — moral law across Hindu/Buddhist/Jain traditions
from posse (to be able) — can/poder/pouvoir/können/potere; deontic modal
from praeteritus (gone by) — past tense marker across languages
from praesens (being present) — present tense marker
from futurus (about to be) — future tense marker across languages
from proximus (nearest) — aqui/here/ici/hier/qui; spatial proximity deictic
from distare (to be apart) — lá/there/là/dort/là; spatial distance deictic
